canonical_definition: An evaluation method where an LLM is prompted to assign a numerical score to individual answers based on their correctness, which are then compared to establish a preference.
papers: DataMan: Data Manager for Pre-training Large Language Models
Varying Shades of Wrong: Aligning LLMs with Wrong Answers Only

definitions.text: An evaluation method where an LLM is prompted to assign a numerical score to individual answers based on their correctness, which are then compared to establish a preference.
definitions.paper: Varying Shades of Wrong: Aligning LLMs with Wrong Answers Only

definitions.text: An evaluation method where each item (e.g., a document) is assigned an absolute score on a predefined scale for a specific criterion.
definitions.paper: DataMan: Data Manager for Pre-training Large Language Models

characterization: An evaluation method where an LLM is prompted to assign a numerical score to individual answers based on their correctness, which are then compared to establish a preference.

iclr25: DataMan: Data Manager for Pre-training Large Language Models
Varying Shades of Wrong: Aligning LLMs with Wrong Answers Only
